workItems.DEPTH_LIMIT_EXCEEDED: Work-item tree depth limit (4 levels) exceeded.
workItems.PARENT_CYCLE: Re-parenting would create a cycle in the work-item tree.
workItems.KEY_CONFLICT: A work item with this key or identifier already exists in the project.
workItems.CROSS_PROJECT_PARENT: A work item parent must belong to the same project as the child.
workItems.REPORTER_NOT_IN_WORKSPACE: The reporter is not a member of this workspace.
workItems.ASSIGNEE_NOT_IN_WORKSPACE: The assignee is not a member of this workspace.
workItems.UNKNOWN_STATUS: Unknown status "{statusKey}" for this project's workflow.
workItems.STALE_WORK_ITEM: This work item was edited by someone else. Refresh and try again.
workItems.TYPE_NOT_ALLOWED_ON_KIND: A type and executor can only be set on a task, subtask, or bug — not on an epic or story.

workflow.NOT_PROJECT_ADMIN: Only a project admin can edit the workflow.
workflow.STATUS_KEY_CONFLICT: A status with key "{key}" already exists in this project.
workflow.STATUS_IN_USE: Status "{statusKey}" is still used by {count} work item(s) and can't be deleted.
workflow.INVALID_REASSIGN_TARGET: Pick a different status in this project to move the items to.
workflow.DEFAULT_STATUS_PROTECTED: "{statusKey}" is a default status — only its color can be changed.
workflow.CANNOT_DELETE_INITIAL_STATUS: Status "{statusKey}" is the initial status and can't be deleted.
workflow.CANNOT_DELETE_LAST_TERMINAL_STATUS: Status "{statusKey}" is the only terminal status — a project needs at least one.

upload.FILE_TOO_LARGE: File is too large — please choose a smaller file.
upload.RATE_LIMITED: Too many uploads — please wait a moment and try again.

customFields.VALUE_TYPE_MISMATCH: The value does not match the field's type.

customFields.OPTION_NOT_IN_FIELD: This option does not belong to the field.

customFields.USER_NOT_ASSIGNABLE: That person can't view this project, so they can't hold this field.
customFields.READ_ONLY: You have read-only access to this project.

labels.LABEL_LIMIT_EXCEEDED: Label limit reached ({limit}) — remove one to add another.
labels.READ_ONLY: You don’t have permission to edit labels.

components.COMPONENT_NAME_CONFLICT: A component with this name already exists.

components.CROSS_PROJECT_COMPONENT: That component belongs to another project.
components.NOT_PROJECT_ADMIN: Only a project admin can manage components.
components.READ_ONLY: You don’t have permission to edit components.

watchers.WATCHERS_FORBIDDEN: You don’t have permission to manage watchers on this work item.
watchers.WATCHER_CANNOT_VIEW: {name} can’t view this work item, so they can’t watch it.

passwordReset.fallback: Or copy this link into your browser:

emailChange.fallback: Or copy this link into your browser:

auth.signInSubhead: Use Motir to turn any product idea into reality.
auth.googleSignInIncomplete: Google sign-in didn't complete. Try again, or use email.
auth.googleSignUpIncomplete: Google sign-up didn't complete. Try again, or use email.

auth.signUpSubhead: Sign up to turn any product idea into reality.
auth.accountExists: An account with this email already exists.
auth.emailHelper: We'll use this to sign you in.
auth.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
auth.somethingWentWrong: Something went wrong. Please try again.
auth.checkInboxSubhead: If an account exists for that email, we've sent a one-time link to reset your password. The link expires in 1 hour.

auth.resetSubhead: Enter the email tied to your account and we'll send a one-time link.
auth.tooManyRequests: Too many reset requests from this device. Please wait an hour and try again.
auth.couldntReachServer: We couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
auth.linkExpiredSubhead: Reset links expire after 1 hour for security. Request a new one to continue.
auth.passwordUpdatedSubhead: You can now sign in with your new password.
auth.setNewPasswordSubhead: Pick something you haven't used before — at least 8 characters.
auth.inviteExpiredSubhead: Invites are valid for 7 days. Ask the inviter for a new link if you'd still like to join.

auth.signInWithInvitedEmail: Sign in with the invited email

project.emptyDescription: Projects group your work items and give them a key like PROD-1. Create one to start planning.
project.identifierTaken: That identifier is already taken. Try a different one.
project.identifierHelper: 3–5 uppercase characters. Work items will be keyed {preview}-1, {preview}-2, …

stub.archivedDescription: Switch to another project to continue working.

createIssue.titlePlaceholder: Summarize the work in a line
createIssue.explanationSkip: Skip this — explanation can be drafted with AI or added after creating.

links.helper: Choose a relationship · links are created together with the work item.

issueViews.heading: Work Items
issueViews.labelsLimitReached: Label limit reached ({limit}) — remove one to add another.
issueViews.epicPrivacyDescription: Private epics stay visible as a row to the public, but their stories, tasks, and progress are hidden from non-members. Members still see everything.
issueViews.epicPrivacyOnHelper: Non-members now see only this epic’s row — its contents are hidden.
issueViews.epicPrivacyAdminOnly: Only project admins can change epic visibility.
issueViews.noProjectListDescription: Pick or create a project from the switcher to track its work items.
issueViews.noProjectDetailDescription: Pick a project from the switcher to view its work items.
issueViews.noProjectEditDescription: Pick a project from the switcher to edit its work items.
issueViews.filterComingSoon: Filtering arrives in a later update

issueViews.noMatchingDescription: No work items match the current filters. Try adjusting or clearing them.
issueViews.quickViewMore: Explanation, relationships, attachments, and the activity feed live on the <link>full page</link>.
issueViews.quickViewUnavailableDescription: {key} may have been moved, deleted, or it’s in a workspace you don’t have access to.
issueViews.archivedSubtitle: Hidden from boards, lists, and search — their history is kept. Restore to bring one back.
issueViews.archivedEmptyDescription: Archived work items show up here. Archive one from its ⋯ menu to hide it from boards and lists while keeping its history.
issueViews.archivedBannerMeta: Archived by {name} · {date} · It’s hidden from boards and lists but kept with its history.
issueViews.archivedBannerRestoreTail: Restore it to bring it back.
issueViews.removeConfirmAfter: ? The work item isn’t deleted — only the link.
issueViews.changedElsewhereRefreshing: This work item changed elsewhere — refreshing.

issueViews.staleBanner: This work item was edited by someone else. Refresh to see the latest.

issueViews.advancedInvalidDescription: The filter in this URL is invalid or from a newer version of Motir, so no filter was applied. Clear it to dismiss this message.
issueViews.filterSupersededTooltip: Managed in Advanced — this filter uses conditions the quick filter can’t show. Open Advanced to edit it.

area.noProjectDescription: Choose a project from the switcher, or create one from the dashboard, to manage its settings.

details.title: Details
details.subtitle: Your project’s name, avatar and key. Changing the key re-keys every work item and keeps old links working. Only project admins can edit these.
details.avatarHelp: A preset icon and colour, shown on the project switcher and lists. Choose “None” to fall back to the key letters.
details.nameHelp: The display name across the app. The URL slug is not affected.
details.keyHelp: Work items are keyed {ident}-1, {ident}-2, … Changing it re-keys every work item and keeps old links working.
details.modalLede: The key prefixes every work item ID in {projectName}. Pick a new one — 3–5 uppercase letters or digits.
details.consequenceIds: Every work item identifier becomes {newKey}-1, {newKey}-2, … — the numbers stay the same.
details.consequenceLinks: Old {oldKey}- links keep working — they redirect to the new key.
details.errKeyFormat: “{value}” is not a valid project key (use 3–5 uppercase letters or digits).
details.errKeyTaken: Another project in this workspace already uses the key “{value}”.
details.errKeyReserved: The key “{value}” is reserved by another project’s previous key.
details.errKeyUnknown: Couldn’t change the project key. Try again.
details.inflightLede: Re-keying every work item to {newKey}-. This is one atomic change — it won’t leave work items half-renamed.
details.successToast: Project key changed to {newKey}. Every work item is now keyed {newKey}, and old {oldKey} links keep working.
details.prevKeysHelp: Old links to these keys redirect to {ident}.
details.releaseBody: Releasing {ident} frees it for other projects and breaks old {ident} links — they’ll stop redirecting and start returning “not found”. This can’t be undone except by changing the key back to {ident}.
details.releasedToast: Released {ident}. It’s free for other projects now, and its old links no longer redirect.
details.releaseErrorTitle: Couldn’t release the key. Try again.

name.heading: Workspace name

members.errorAlreadyMember: {email} is already a member of this workspace.
members.errorRateLimited: You've already sent 3 invites to this address in the last hour. Please wait before trying again.

members.errorUnexpected: Something went wrong. Please try again.

danger.heading: Danger zone
danger.leaveWorkspaceDesc: You'll lose access to all data in this workspace.
danger.lastMemberTooltip: You're the last member — delete the workspace instead.
danger.deleteWorkspaceDesc: Permanently delete this workspace and all its data. This cannot be undone.
danger.deleteModalDesc: This will permanently delete the workspace and all its data (projects, work items, members). This action cannot be undone.

empty.title: No project yet
empty.description: Create a project from the switcher in the top-left to get started.

project.title: Project settings
project.subtitle: Configure the active project and manage its lifecycle.

archive.title: Archive project
archive.description: Hides this project from the switcher and lists. Work items and history are preserved.
archive.modalDesc: Archiving hides this project from the switcher and lists. Its work items and history are preserved — you can restore the project later. This does not delete any data.

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.cardDescription: The statuses a work item can hold in this project, and the legal moves between them.

empty.description: Create a project from the switcher in the top-left to manage its workflow.

workflow.pageDescription: The statuses a work item can hold in {projectName}, and the legal moves between them. {editHint}
workflow.editHintReader: Only a project admin can edit these.
workflow.policyOpenDesc: Open mode: any status can transition to any other.
workflow.policyRestrictedDesc: Restricted mode: only the transitions below are allowed.
workflow.tableCaption: Each cell is a legal move from the row status to the column status.
workflow.restoreModalDesc: This re-adds the standard transitions (the allowed moves between statuses) that are missing from this project's workflow. It does not remove any transitions you added, and it doesn't touch your statuses — nothing you've set up is lost.
workflow.reassignAffected: {count, plural, one {# work item still use this status. Choose a status to move it to — then “{label}” is removed.} other {# work items still use this status. Choose a status to move them to — then “{label}” is removed.}}
workflow.defaultStatusNote: <name></name> is a default status — only its color can be changed.

board.title: Board
board.cardDescription: Columns and which statuses each column shows.
board.subtitle: Columns, status mapping, swimlanes and WIP limits for the board below. Each board has its own configuration.
board.notFoundDescription: This board doesn’t exist in this project, or you don’t have access to it. Pick a board from the switcher above.
board.noBoardDescription: This project doesn’t have a board configured yet, so there are no columns to manage.
board.boardNameHint: Shown in the board header on the Boards page.
board.unmappedHint: Drag onto a column, or use a column’s “Add status” menu.
board.unmappedEmpty: Every status is on the board — nothing is hidden.
board.unmappedDropAria: Unmap a status by dropping it here
board.columnEmpty: No statuses yet — map one from the rail.
board.readOnlyBanner: You have read-only access to board settings. Ask a project admin to add, rename, or remap columns.
board.deleteBody: Its mapped statuses return to Unmapped statuses — work items keep their status and are not deleted; they’re just hidden from the board until the status is mapped again.
board.deleteNoStatuses: This column maps no statuses, so nothing returns to Unmapped.
board.guardBody: Its mapped statuses still hold {count} work items on the board. Map those statuses to another column first, then delete this one. No work item is ever removed by a board change.
board.toastMapError: Couldn’t map {status} to that column. It was returned.
board.toastUnmapError: Couldn’t unmap {status}. It was returned to the column.
board.toastAddColumnError: Couldn’t add the column. Try again.
board.toastRenameColumnError: Couldn’t rename the column. Try again.
board.toastReorderColumnError: Couldn’t reorder the column. The order was restored.
board.toastDeleteColumnError: Couldn’t delete the column. Try again.
board.toastLastColumnError: A board must keep at least one column.
board.toastColumnNotEmptyError: “{name}” still holds work items — remap its statuses first.
board.toastRenameBoardError: Couldn’t rename the board. The previous name was restored.
board.errorDescription: Something went wrong loading this board’s configuration. Try again.

estimation.title: Estimation
estimation.cardDescription: Set the estimation statistic and point scale for this project.
estimation.pageDescription: How this project sizes work — the planning estimate the backlog, board, and roll-ups display.

empty.description: Create a project to configure its estimation settings.

estimation.cardSubtitle: Applies to every board and sprint in this project.
estimation.statisticHint: Which measure is THE estimate that rolls up to sprints and epics.
estimation.scaleHint: The suggested deck the estimate picker offers. Any number is still allowed — the deck only suggests.
estimation.customHint: The deck the picker offers. Values must be positive numbers.
estimation.savedDesc: Your changes apply to every board and sprint.
estimation.saveError: Your estimation settings weren't saved. Please try again.
estimation.readOnlyBanner: Only project admins can change estimation settings.

customFields.cardDescription: Custom fields that work items in this project can carry — like severity, customer, or a go-live date.
customFields.title: Fields
customFields.subtitle: Custom fields that work items in {projectName} can carry, alongside the built-in ones. Fields belong to this project only.
customFields.fieldCapTooltip: This project has reached the {max}-field limit
customFields.fieldCapInfo: A project can hold up to {max} custom fields. Delete a field to add another.

typeDesc.select: One option from a list you manage.
typeDesc.user: A person who can view this project.

customFields.glossUsage: used on {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}
customFields.emptyDescription: Custom fields capture project-specific details — like severity, customer, or a go-live date — on every work item in this project.
customFields.labelHelper: Shown on work items and in filters.
customFields.labelRequired: Enter a label for the field.
customFields.typeHelper: The type can't be changed after the field is created.
customFields.optionCapReached: {count} / {max} — a field can hold up to {max} options
customFields.optionUsage: used on {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}
customFields.optionInUseTooltip: In use on {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}} — archive instead
customFields.deleteFieldBody: Deletes the field and its values on {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}. This can't be undone.
customFields.deleteFieldBodyNoValues: Deletes the field. No work items hold a value for it.
customFields.readOnlyNote: Only project admins can manage fields.
customFields.errorDescription: Something went wrong on our side. Try again.
customFields.toastCreateError: The field wasn't created. Please try again.
customFields.toastSaveError: Your changes weren't saved. Please try again.
customFields.toastReorderError: The new order wasn't saved. Please try again.
customFields.toastDeleteError: The field wasn't deleted. Please try again.
customFields.toastOptionError: The option change wasn't saved. Please try again.
customFields.toastOptionInUseError: That option is in use on work items — archive it instead.
customFields.toastFieldCapError: This project has reached the {max}-field limit.
customFields.toastOptionCapError: A field can hold up to {max} options.

components.cardDescription: The parts of the product work items belong to — like API, Web, or Billing — each with an optional default assignee.
components.title: Components
components.subtitle: The parts of {projectName} that work items can belong to. A work item can carry several components; components belong to this project only.
components.usage: {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}
components.emptyDescription: Components group work items by part of the product — like API, Web, or Billing. New work items created with a component can pick up its default assignee automatically.
components.nameRequired: Enter a name for the component.
components.nameConflict: A component named “{name}” already exists in this project.
components.descriptionPlaceholder: What part of the product this covers…
components.defaultAssigneeHelper: New work items created with this component and no assignee are assigned to this person. Existing work items are never changed.
components.pickerScopeNote: Only people who can view this project are listed.
components.deleteInUseBody: {name} is on {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}. Choose what happens to them — the work items themselves are untouched.
components.deleteChoiceLabel: What happens to the work items
components.moveChoice: Move {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}} to…
components.moveChoiceGloss: Their {name} association is replaced. Items already carrying the target keep one.
components.removeChoice: Remove the component from {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}
components.deleteUnusedBody: No work items carry this component. This removes it from the project.
components.readOnlyNote: Only project admins can manage components.
components.errorDescription: Something went wrong on our side. Try again.
components.toastCreateError: The component wasn't created. Please try again.
components.toastSaveError: Your changes weren't saved. Please try again.
components.toastDeleteError: The component wasn't deleted. Please try again.

access.title: Access & members
access.cardDescription: Who can find, view, and edit this project, and the people on it.
access.subtitle: Control who can see and edit {projectName}. Workspace owners and admins always have access.
access.accessSubheading: Who in the {workspaceName} workspace can reach this project.

levelDesc.open: Any member of the {workspaceName} workspace can view and edit this project.
levelDesc.limited: Any workspace member can view and comment, but only project members can edit.
levelDesc.private: Only people added to the project below can find or open it. Hidden from everyone else.
levelDesc.public: Publish this project to the web as a build-in-public page — anyone can follow the board, roadmap, and work items without an account, indexable by search engines. Visitors sign in only to submit, upvote, or comment. It's the same public access level, presented as a status you can start and stop anytime.

access.goPrivateNote: The {count, plural, one {# person} other {# people}} who can currently access {projectName} will be added as project members so no one loses access. You can adjust roles or remove them in the Members list.
access.addMemberEmpty: No workspace members left to add.
access.addMemberHint: Members already on the project are hidden.
access.readOnlyNote: Only project admins can add members or change access.
access.errorLastAdmin: This is the only project admin — assign another admin first.
access.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

buildInPublic.confirmLead: Your project becomes a public page anyone on the web can open — no account needed, and search engines can index it. Here's exactly what changes:
buildInPublic.bulletBoard: Your board and roadmap — status, progress, and what's shipping.
buildInPublic.bulletItems: Your work items — titles, types, and descriptions, crawlable by search engines.
buildInPublic.bulletSignIn: Visitors sign in only to submit a request, upvote, or comment — they can't edit anything.
buildInPublic.bulletPrivate: Stays private: assignees, estimates, and internal comments are never shown.
buildInPublic.reassurance: You can stop building in public anytime — the page goes offline and the public link stops working. Submitted requests and upvotes are kept.
buildInPublic.promoSub: Turn the project into a public build-in-public page.
buildInPublic.promoBullet1: Share your board, roadmap, and work items with anyone — no account needed, indexable by search engines.
buildInPublic.promoBullet2: Let people submit, upvote, and comment on requests — they sign in only to act.
buildInPublic.promoBullet3: Internal fields stay private — assignees, estimates, and internal comments are never shown.
buildInPublic.stopConfirmLead: The project goes back to its previous access. Here's what happens:
buildInPublic.stopBulletOffline: The public page goes offline — visitors and search engines can no longer open it.
buildInPublic.stopBulletLink: The public link stops working until you build in public again.
buildInPublic.stopBulletKept: Submitted requests, upvotes, and comments are kept — nothing is deleted.
buildInPublic.stopReassurance: You can start building in public again anytime from Settings.

public.linkSubheading: Share this project with anyone — no account needed to view.
public.linkNote: Anyone can open this link with no sign-in, and the page is crawlable by search engines. Visitors sign in only to submit, upvote, or comment. To stop sharing, set the access level above to anything other than Building in public.
public.heroOverviewSubheading: Edit the tagline, tags, and README right on the public page — what you change is what visitors see.
public.heroOverviewNote: Opens the public Overview with the on-page editor. It's hidden while the project isn't public.

jobs.title: Job runs
jobs.subtitle: Background jobs for this workspace — delivery of invites & password resets, and any scheduled work. Failed jobs land in the dead-letter queue, where an owner can replay them.
jobs.replayTooltip: Only a workspace owner can replay jobs
jobs.dlqDetailDesc: The failure and the original event payload (replayable as-is).
jobs.dlqEmptyDesc: Every job has succeeded or is still retrying.
jobs.runsEmptyDesc: When a background job runs, it'll appear here.

notifications.heading: Notifications
notifications.helper: Choose how you're notified for each kind of update. These apply to you across every workspace.

mentioned.label: Mentioned
mentioned.desc: Someone @-mentions you in a comment or description.

commented.label: Commented on an item you're involved in
commented.desc: A new comment on an item you reported, are assigned, or commented on.

assigned.label: Assigned to you
assigned.desc: An item is assigned to you.

transitioned.label: An item you're watching changes status
transitioned.desc: Someone changes the status of an item you watch (including moves to done or back open).

notifications.defaultsNote: Direct events — mentions, comments on your items, and assignments — default on for both channels. An untouched row uses that default (it is not off until you turn it off).

language.heading: Language & region
language.subtitle: The language Motir's interface is shown in — menus, labels and dates. Personal to you, applied on every device you sign in on.

displayLanguage.label: Display language
displayLanguage.desc: Menus, labels and dates use this language.

profile.heading: Profile
profile.subtitle: Your personal details — the name and photo teammates see across Motir, and the email and password you sign in with. Changes apply to your account everywhere you're signed in.

card.title: Profile
card.subtitle: How you appear to your team.

photo.label: Photo
photo.desc: PNG or JPG, up to 2 MB. Shown on your avatar, comments and mentions.

removeConfirm.title: Remove photo?
removeConfirm.body: Your avatar will revert to your initials.

errors.invalidType: Choose a PNG or JPG image.
errors.tooLarge: Image must be 2 MB or smaller.
errors.failed: Couldn't update your photo. Try again.

name.label: Name
name.desc: Shown on your work, comments and mentions.

email.label: Email
email.desc: Used to sign in and to send you notifications.

modal.title: Change email
modal.description: We'll send a confirmation link to the new address. Your email changes only after you confirm from that inbox.

errors.taken: That email is already in use by another account.
errors.rateLimited: Too many requests. Please try again in a little while.
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

toast.title: Confirmation sent
toast.body: Check {email} to confirm your new address.

card.title: Password & security
card.subtitle: The password you use to sign in with email.

password.label: Password
password.desc: Used to sign in with your email address.

modal.title: Change password
modal.desc: Enter your current password, then choose a new one.

errors.tooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment and try again.
errors.noCredential: This account signs in with Google and has no password to change.
errors.alreadyHasPassword: This account already has a password. Use change password instead.

changed.title: Password updated
changed.desc: Use your new password next time you sign in.

linkSent.title: Reset link sent
linkSent.desc: Check your inbox for a link to set a password.

oauth.body: You sign in with Google. Send yourself a reset link to set a password and also sign in with email.
oauth.sent: Reset link sent — check your inbox to set a password.

appearance.heading: Appearance
appearance.subtitle: Theme Motir to your taste — its colour, type and shape. Everything applies live across the whole app, and saves to your account so it follows you to every device.

card.title: Theme & design
card.subtitle: Pick a theme, style, palette and type pairing. The whole app re-skins instantly — this page included.

sync.error: Couldn't sync to your account — your changes are saved on this device.

theme.name: Theme
theme.help: Light, dark, or match your device.

note.system: Follows your device's light / dark setting.

style.name: Style
style.help: The shape and feel — silhouette, elevation, density.

palette.name: Palette
palette.help: The colour scheme the interface wears.

type.name: Typography
type.help: The typeface pairing for headings and body.

showcase.label: Live preview of the current theme, style, palette and type

automation.title: Automation
automation.subtitle: When something happens in {projectName}, run actions automatically. Rules run as their owner — only project admins can manage them.
automation.capReached: {max} of {max} rules — delete a rule to add another.

empty.title: No rules yet
empty.description: Automate repetitive work — transition items, set fields, add watchers or comments when something happens. Rules run as their owner.

noAccess.title: Admins only
noAccess.description: Project automation is managed by project admins. Ask an admin if you need a rule changed.

automation.autoDisabledBanner: {name} was disabled automatically after {count} consecutive failures. Fix the rule and re-enable it — re-enabling resets the failure count.

delete.title: Delete {name}?
delete.body: This permanently deletes the rule and its run history. This can't be undone.

editor.ifSub: conditions on the triggering item — optional
editor.thenSub: actions run in order, as the rule owner
editor.actionCapReached: {max} of {max} actions — the maximum for one rule.

toast.saveError: Couldn't save the rule. Try again.
toast.deleteError: Couldn't delete the rule. Try again.
toast.toggleError: Couldn't update the rule. Try again.
toast.capError: You've reached the {max}-rule limit for this project.

log.title: Run history — {ruleName}
log.retention: Run history is kept for 90 days.
log.loadError: Couldn't load run history. Try again.

empty.title: No runs yet
empty.description: This rule hasn't fired. When an item matches its trigger and conditions, each run appears here with its result, duration and the item that triggered it.

apiTokens.heading: API tokens
apiTokens.subtitle: Personal access tokens let your coding agents reach Motir over the MCP server. Each token acts as you — give every agent its own, and revoke any you no longer use.

card.title: Your tokens
card.subtitle: Shown once at creation, then stored only as a hash.

scopes.permissionsHelper: Choose what this token is allowed to do. You can grant less than your own access, never more.

read.name: Read everything
read.desc: View work items, boards, sprints, comments, and reports.

sprintsWrite.name: Manage sprints
sprintsWrite.desc: Create sprints and move work items in and out of them.

integration.name: Connect integrations
integration.desc: Link external tools and post activity back to Motir.

workItemsWrite.name: Edit work items
workItemsWrite.desc: Create and update work items, comments, links, and attachments.

workItemsArchive.name: Archive work items
workItemsArchive.desc: Archive and restore work items. This can be undone.

workItemsDelete.name: Delete work items
workItemsDelete.desc: Permanently delete a work item and its entire subtree. This can't be undone.

scopes.emptyError: Grant at least one permission to create a token.

apiTokens.expiresIn: in {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}

createModal.title: Create API token
createModal.description: The token will be shown once, right after you create it. Store it somewhere safe.
createModal.labelHelper: A name to recognise this token by — e.g. the agent or machine using it.
createModal.scopeHelper: This token can only access the workspace you choose here.
createModal.expiresHelper: After this, the token stops working and agents must be re-issued one.
createModal.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
createModal.copyFailed: Couldn't copy — select and copy the token manually.

created.title: Token created
created.description: Copy your token now — for security, Motir won't show it again.
created.warning: This is the only time you'll see this token. If you lose it, revoke it and create a new one.

revokeConfirm.title: Revoke "{label}"?
revokeConfirm.body: Any agent using this token loses access to Motir immediately. This can't be undone — you'll need to create a new token to reconnect.
revokeConfirm.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

empty.title: No API tokens yet
empty.body: API tokens let your coding agents talk to Motir over the MCP server — list ready work, open work items, move them, and comment, all as you.

toast.title: Token copied
toast.body: Paste it into your agent's MCP config now — it won't be shown again.

boards.heading: Boards
boards.subtitle: Kanban board for {project}
boards.noProjectDescription: Pick or create a project from the switcher to see its board.
boards.errorDescription: Something went wrong loading this board. Try again.
boards.noBoardDescription: This project doesn’t have a board configured yet.
boards.wipHint: A non-negative whole number. Soft limit — it warns when exceeded but never blocks a drop. Clear to remove the limit.
boards.wipOverLimitAria: {count} of {limit} work items — over the WIP limit
boards.wipSaveErrorDescription: The WIP limit for {column} wasn’t saved. Try again.
boards.dndInstructions: To pick up a card, press Space. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the card. Press Space again to drop, or Escape to cancel.
boards.announcementPickedUp: Picked up {key}. Over column {col}, position {index} of {count}.
boards.announcementMoved: {key} moved to {col}, position {index} of {count}. Press Space to drop, Escape to cancel.
boards.moveUnmappedDescription: That column maps no status. {key} returned.
boards.moveErrorDescription: Couldn’t move {key}. It was returned to its place.
boards.emptyDescription: This board doesn’t have any work items yet. Create one and it’ll show up here.
boards.filteredEmptyTitle: No work items match this filter
boards.filteredEmptyDescription: No work items on this board match the active filter. Clear it or adjust the conditions to see them.
boards.unmappedText: These statuses aren’t on this board — work items in them are hidden here:
boards.overCapText: This board has more than {cap, number} work items — refine the board filter to see them all. Only the first {cap, number} are shown.
boards.groupByErrorDescription: Couldn’t change the swimlane grouping. The board is unchanged.
boards.reassignErrorDescription: Couldn’t update {key}. The change was reverted.
boards.lastBoardNote: A project keeps at least one board, so its last board can’t be deleted.
boards.newBoardSeedHint: New boards seed default columns from the project workflow, so they’re usable right away.
boards.deleteBoardBody: This removes the board and its column & swimlane configuration.
boards.deleteBoardCalloutBody: Work items belong to the project, not a board — they stay on Motir and remain visible on your other boards.
boards.boardCreateErrorDescription: The board couldn’t be created. Please try again.
boards.boardRenameErrorDescription: The board couldn’t be renamed. The change was reverted.
boards.boardSetDefaultErrorDescription: Couldn’t set the default board. The change was reverted.
boards.boardDeleteErrorDescription: The board couldn’t be deleted. The change was reverted.
boards.sprintDaysRemaining: {days, plural, one {# day remaining} other {# days remaining}}
boards.sprintEndedTitle: Past the end date — complete this sprint
boards.sprintCompleteLoadErrorDescription: We couldn’t load the sprint details. Try again.
boards.noActiveSprintDescription: This board has no sprint in progress. Plan and start a sprint from the backlog, and its work items will appear here in workflow columns.

ready.heading: Ready to start
ready.subtitle: {project} · {key}
ready.manualTooltip: A human task — no run command

empty.title: No instruction yet
empty.body: This human task has no description. Add one on the work item so whoever picks it up knows what to do.

ready.noProjectDescription: Create or select a project to see what’s ready to start.

popover.title: What is "ready"?
popover.body1: A work item is ready when every work item blocking it has been completed — so it has no unfinished blockers and can be started right now.
popover.body2: Click Copy on any row to put its motir run PROD-… command on your clipboard, then paste it into your own coding agent to dispatch the work.

empty.title: Nothing's ready right now
empty.body: A work item appears here once every work item blocking it is done. Right now nothing is fully unblocked — head to Work Items to see what's still in progress and what it's waiting on.

backlog.heading: Backlog
backlog.subtitle: {project} · plan & groom — rank, sprint, estimate
backlog.noProjectDescription: Select or create a project to plan its backlog.
backlog.filteredEmptyTitle: No work items match this filter
backlog.filteredEmptyDescription: Try removing a condition, or clear the filter to see the full backlog.
backlog.sprintFilterEmpty: No work item in this sprint matches the active filter.
backlog.backlogRegionLabel: Backlog, {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}
backlog.issueCount: {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}
backlog.emptyDescription: Every work item is assigned to a sprint. Create a work item to add to the backlog.
backlog.errorDescription: Something went wrong fetching this project's backlog. Check your connection and try again.
backlog.sprintsErrorDescription: Something went wrong fetching this project's sprints. Check your connection and try again.
backlog.allLoaded: All {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}} loaded
backlog.selectionBarLabel: {count, plural, one {# work item selected} other {# work items selected}}
backlog.noSprintsToMove: No sprints to move to. Create one first.
backlog.bulkErrorDescription: The selection couldn't be moved. It's back where it was — please try again.
backlog.createIssueErrorDescription: Something went wrong creating the work item. Please try again.
backlog.sprintRegionLabel: {name}, {state}, {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}
backlog.sprintEmpty: No work items — drag from the backlog, or use + Create work item.
backlog.startSprintEmpty: Add at least one work item to start the sprint.

startSprintFlow.title: Start sprint
startSprintFlow.subtitle: {sprint} · {project}
startSprintFlow.derivedWindow: {start} → {end} · {days, plural, one {ends in # day} other {ends in # days}}
startSprintFlow.windowInvalid: End date must be after the start date
startSprintFlow.committedSummary: {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}} · {points, plural, =0 {—} one {# point} other {# points}} committed at start
startSprintFlow.alreadyActive: {project} already has an active sprint ({sprint}). Complete it first — a project can run one active sprint at a time.
startSprintFlow.alreadyActiveGeneric: {project} already has an active sprint. Complete it first — a project can run one active sprint at a time.
startSprintFlow.errorDescription: Something went wrong starting the sprint. Please try again.

backlog.createSprintErrorDescription: Something went wrong creating the sprint. Please try again.
backlog.moveErrorDescription: {key} couldn't be moved. It's back where it was — please try again.
backlog.dndInstructions: Press Space to pick up a work item, the arrow keys to move it between sprints and the backlog, Space again to drop, or Escape to cancel.

completeSprintFlow.title: Complete sprint
completeSprintFlow.subtitle: {sprint} · {project}
completeSprintFlow.loadError: Couldn't load the sprint summary. Please try again.
completeSprintFlow.completedPointsUnestimated: {completed, plural, =0 {no points estimated} other {# points completed}}
completeSprintFlow.carryOverPoints: {points, plural, =0 {nothing to carry over} one {# point carries over} other {# points carry over}}
completeSprintFlow.chooserLabel: Move the {count, plural, one {# incomplete work item} other {# incomplete work items}} to
completeSprintFlow.carryBacklogSub: Unfinished work items return to the backlog in rank order
completeSprintFlow.carrySprintSub: Roll the unfinished work items into a planned sprint
completeSprintFlow.noPlannedSprints: No planned sprint to roll into — create one in the backlog first.
completeSprintFlow.allComplete: All work items are complete — nothing to carry over.
completeSprintFlow.errorDescription: Something went wrong completing the sprint. Please try again.

sprintReport.heading: Sprint report
sprintReport.title: {name} report
sprintReport.scopeChange: {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}} added after the sprint started (scope change)
sprintReport.emptyCompleted: No work items were completed in this sprint.
sprintReport.burndownDescActive: In-sprint burndown, day {day} of {total}. The guideline falls from {committed} committed {statistic} to 0; actual remaining as of today is {remaining}.
sprintReport.burndownDescComplete: Completed-sprint burndown over {total} days. The guideline falls from {committed} committed {statistic} to 0; the sprint ended with {remaining} remaining.
sprintReport.burndownTableCaption: Burndown — {statistic} remaining by day (end of day).
sprintReport.burndownEmptySprint: Nothing committed — the guideline is flat at 0.
sprintReport.burndownErrorBody: Something went wrong fetching the report. Try again.
sprintReport.burndownSubPoints: {committed, plural, one {# pt committed} other {# pts committed}}
sprintReport.burndownSubIssues: {committed, plural, one {# item committed} other {# items committed}}
sprintReport.velocityWindow: Last {count, plural, one {# completed sprint} other {# completed sprints}}
sprintReport.velocityLowHistoryBody: Velocity needs at least 2 completed sprints to show a trend. Complete a sprint to start the chart.
sprintReport.velocityDesc: Velocity over the last {count} completed sprints. Average completed: {average}.
sprintReport.velocityTableCaption: Velocity — committed vs completed {statistic} per sprint. Average completed: {average}.

estimation.saveError: Couldn't update the estimate. Please try again.

savedFilters.heading: Filters
savedFilters.subtitle: Saved filters for {project}. Save new ones from Work Items.

visibilityHint.private: Only you and project admins can see it.
visibilityHint.project: Everyone who can browse this project can see and apply it.

savedFilters.applyError: This filter can't be applied — its saved criteria are no longer valid.
savedFilters.starError: Couldn't update your star. Please try again.

pager.summary: {count, plural, one {# filter} other {# filters}} · page {page} of {pages}

empty.title: No saved filters yet
empty.description: Build a filter on Work Items and save it to reuse and share it here.

error.title: Couldn't load filters
error.description: Something went wrong while loading this project's filters.

edit.title: Edit filter details
edit.viewerNote: Sharing needs the Member role — as a viewer, your filters stay private to you.
edit.goPrivateNote: Making this private hides it from everyone else — their subscriptions stop delivering, and dashboard widgets using it show “Filter missing”.
edit.duplicateName: A filter named “{name}” already exists in this project.
edit.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

changeOwnerDialog.title: Change owner of “{name}”
changeOwnerDialog.description: The new owner must be able to browse this project.
changeOwnerDialog.errorInvalidOwner: That member can't browse this project.
changeOwnerDialog.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

deleteDialog.title: Delete filter?
deleteDialog.body: This permanently deletes “{name}” for everyone in this project.
deleteDialog.subscriptions: {count, plural, one {# subscription} other {# subscriptions}} will be removed.
deleteDialog.noDependents: No subscriptions depend on this filter.
deleteDialog.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

dropdown.label: Saved
dropdown.trigger: Saved filters — apply, star, or search
dropdown.emptyStarred: No starred filters yet — star a filter to pin it here.
dropdown.emptyMine: You haven't saved any filters yet.
dropdown.emptyProject: No filters shared with this project yet.
dropdown.error: Couldn't load filters. Please try again.

save.title: Save filter
save.duplicateName: A filter named “{name}” already exists in this project.
save.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

applied.nonOwnerTooltip: Only the owner ({owner}) can overwrite this filter — Save as creates your own copy.
applied.chipAriaShared: Applied filter: {name} (shared with project) — open saved filters

subscribe.title: Subscribe to “{name}”
subscribe.loadError: Couldn't load your subscription. Please try again.
subscribe.explainer: Emails you the first 50 results and the total count, on the workspace timezone (UTC). A report, not an alert — it sends even when nothing matches.
subscribe.saveError: Couldn't update your subscription. Please try again.

confirm.title: Unsubscribe from this filter?
confirm.body: You'll stop receiving scheduled result emails for this saved filter.

done.title: Unsubscribed
done.body: You won't receive any more emails for this filter. You can re-subscribe anytime from the Filters directory.

invalid.title: This link isn’t valid
invalid.body: This unsubscribe link is malformed or has already been used. If you're still receiving emails, unsubscribe from the Filters directory.

hub.title: Reports
hub.subtitle: Built-in analysis of this project's work items, sprints, and throughput.
hub.burndownBody: Points remaining across a sprint, with a sprint picker.
hub.velocityBody: Committed vs completed points over recent sprints.
hub.sprintReportBody: Scope, completed, and carried-over work for a sprint.
hub.createdVsResolvedBody: How fast work items are opened versus closed over a window.
hub.distributionBody: Break work items down by status, assignee, priority, type, and more.
hub.averageAgeBody: How old your unresolved work items are getting, over time.
hub.resolutionTimeBody: How long work items take to resolve — is the team speeding up?
hub.workloadBody: Open work per assignee — who's carrying how much.
hub.noProjectBody: Create a project to see its reports.

states.noAccessBody: This data is scoped to a project you can't view.
states.emptyBody: No matching work items for the selected scope and window.
states.staleFilterBody: The saved filter behind this report was deleted or is no longer visible.
states.staleStatisticBody: The custom field this report grouped by was deleted.

cvr.title: Created vs Resolved
cvr.chartDesc: Created vs resolved per {period} over the last {count} buckets. Created {created} total versus resolved {resolved} total; the difference is shaded red where the backlog grows and green where the team catches up.
cvr.tableCaption: Created vs resolved per period (per-period counts).
cvr.tableCaptionCumulative: Created vs resolved per period (cumulative running totals).

distribution.title: Status distribution
distribution.itemsCount: {count, plural, one {# work item} other {# work items}}

burndown.title: Burndown chart
burndown.noSprintsBody: Start a sprint from the backlog to see its burndown.

averageAge.title: Average age
averageAge.chartDesc: Average age of unresolved work items per {period} over the last {count} buckets. The window average is {average} days.
averageAge.tableCaption: Average age of unresolved work items per period (days).
averageAge.emptyBody: Every work item in this window is resolved, or none were created yet. Widen the date range or pick another scope.

resolutionTime.title: Resolution time
resolutionTime.legendUnit: avg days, work items resolved in period
resolutionTime.chartDesc: Average resolution time per {period} over the last {count} buckets. The window average is {average} days.
resolutionTime.tableCaption: Average resolution time per period (days), by resolution date.
resolutionTime.emptyBody: No work items were resolved in this window. Widen the date range or pick another scope.

workload.title: Workload
workload.subPoints: open work by assignee · {points} story points across {count} work items
workload.subCount: open work by assignee · {count} work items
workload.legendUnitPoints: unresolved story points per assignee · ranked
workload.legendUnitCount: unresolved work items per assignee · ranked
workload.chartDesc: Open {measure} per assignee, ranked: {breakdown}. Total {total} across {count} unresolved work items.
workload.tableCaption: Open (unresolved) work per assignee — story points and work-item count.
workload.emptyBody: Every unresolved work item is unassigned, or there's no open work. Pick another scope.

dashboards.title: Dashboards
dashboards.subtitle: Named, shareable grids of widgets over your projects and saved filters.

empty.title: Create your first dashboard
empty.body: Dashboards collect widgets — work item tables, charts, and reports — over your projects and saved filters into one shareable view.

create.title: New dashboard
create.privateDesc: Only you can see and edit it.
create.workspaceDesc: Everyone in the workspace can view it. You edit.

deleteModal.title: Delete {name}?
deleteModal.body: This removes the dashboard and its widgets for everyone. This can’t be undone.

grid.capReachedNote: {max} / {max} widgets — remove one to add another
grid.emptyBody: Add a widget to start building this dashboard.
grid.emptyViewerBody: The owner hasn’t added any widgets to this dashboard.
grid.ownerMeta: Owned by you · {count, plural, one {# widget} other {# widgets}}
grid.sharedMeta: Owned by {owner} · {count, plural, one {# widget} other {# widgets}}

states.errorBody: Something went wrong fetching this widget’s data.
states.noAccessBody: This widget points at a project you can’t view. Counts and rows stay hidden.
states.staleBody: The saved filter this widget used was deleted, so it can’t load results.
states.capBody: A dashboard holds up to {max} widgets. Remove one to add another.

add.title: Add a widget
add.filter_results_desc: A paginated work item table from a saved filter or project (up to 50 per page).
add.distribution_desc: A donut breaking work items down by status, assignee, priority, type, and more.
add.created_vs_resolved_desc: Two-series trend of work items created against work items resolved over time.
add.average_age_desc: A bar of how old your unresolved work items are getting, per period.
add.resolution_time_desc: A bar of how long work items take to resolve, per period.
add.workload_desc: Ranked bars of open work per assignee — who's carrying how much.

dashboards.donutDesc: Donut of {total} work items grouped by {statistic}.
dashboards.donutEmpty: No work items to chart yet.
dashboards.cvrDesc: Work items created versus resolved per {period} over the last {days} days.
dashboards.barDescAge: Average age of unresolved work items per {period} over the last {days} days.
dashboards.barDescResolution: Average resolution time per {period} over the last {days} days.
dashboards.hbarEmpty: No open work to chart yet.

empty.title: You're all caught up
empty.body: No notifications yet. Mentions, comments, and assignments will land here.

error.title: Couldn't load notifications
error.body: Something went wrong fetching your feed.

settings.title: Organization settings
settings.subtitle: Manage the {org} organization — the account your workspaces live under. Only organization owners and admins can change these.
settings.generalSub: The organization’s identity across all its workspaces.
settings.urlHint: Used in links to this organization. Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens.
settings.billingSub: Plan, credits and usage roll up to the organization.
settings.billingNote: Billing, credits and usage for this organization live here — added in a later release. No billing controls are active yet; this placeholder marks where they’ll sit so the layout stays stable.
settings.workspaceConfigSub: Folded in here while you have one workspace — splits into its own area when you add a second.
settings.workspaceConfigNote: Workflows & statuses, custom fields, labels, components, automation and dashboards are workspace-scoped — they live on the workspace underneath (there is no org→workspace config inheritance). With one workspace they’re managed in workspace settings; add a second and they split into a per-workspace area, with the new workspace seeded by copying this one’s config so it looks inherited.
settings.deleteOrgNote: Deleting the organization removes all its workspaces and data. This cannot be undone.

members.title: Members
members.subtitle: Everyone in the {org} organization, across all its workspaces. An organization role applies org-wide; workspace membership is shown per person.

roles.ownerDesc: Full control — billing, delete, all workspaces
roles.memberDesc: Belongs to the org; access by workspace role

roleHelp.adminLine: Owner / Admin administer the organization and are granted admin on every workspace under it — above any per-workspace role.
roleHelp.memberLine: An org Member belongs to the org but has no cross-workspace powers — what they can do inside a workspace is still governed by their workspace role.
roleHelp.gatingNote: Org membership gates workspace access: someone removed from the organization loses access to every workspace under it.

inviteModal.title: Invite to {org}
inviteModal.description: Add an existing Motir teammate to this organization and choose their org role. People who aren’t on Motir yet join by accepting a workspace invite — they’re enrolled in the organization automatically.
inviteModal.errorNotFound: No Motir account for {email}. Invite them to a workspace first — they’ll join the organization automatically.
inviteModal.errorGeneric: Couldn’t add that person. Try again.

states.emptyDescription: Invite teammates to the {org} organization. They’ll get access to the workspaces you add them to.
states.errorDescription: Something went wrong fetching this organization’s members. Try again.
states.forbiddenDescription: Only owners and admins of {org} can manage members and settings. Ask an organization admin if you need a change.
states.noActiveOrgDescription: You don’t belong to an organization yet.

triage.heading: Triage
triage.subtitle: {project} · {key} — incoming bug reports & feature requests, excluded from the tree until you act
triage.noAccessDescription: You don’t have access to triage for this project.
triage.loadError: Couldn’t load the triage queue. Please try again.
triage.selectPromptBody: Pick a row from the queue to see its full report and act on it.
triage.memberMeta: Team member · reported from the in-app widget

empty.title: No items to triage
empty.description: New bug reports and feature requests land here — from the in-app “Report” button or a signed-in “Submit a request” on the public project page.

promote.heading: Promote into
promote.sprintSub: Add to a sprint in progress

merge.heading: Mark as a duplicate of
merge.note: Folds comments & attachments into {key} and cancels this submission.

toast.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

widget.heading: Report something
widget.descriptionPlaceholder: Add any details that help us reproduce or understand it…
widget.submittedDetail: Filed as {key}, now waiting in triage.
widget.error: Couldn’t submit your report. Please try again.

publicProjects.bannerBody: Anyone can view it — no account needed. Sign in to submit, upvote, or comment on requests.
publicProjects.viewOnly: View-only — you can’t edit work items
publicProjects.ctaBody: Tell us what to build next. It takes a minute and goes straight to the team.
publicProjects.autoIntroTagline: A public project on Motir — view the board, the work items, and the roadmap. Sign in to submit, upvote, or comment on requests.
publicProjects.signInToActTitle: Sign in to submit a request
publicProjects.signInToActBody: Reading is open to everyone; posting needs a Motir account.
publicProjects.submitFormSubtitle: Tell the Motir team about a bug or a feature you’d like.
publicProjects.descriptionPlaceholder: What should it do, and why does it matter to you?
publicProjects.submittedAs: Submitted as {name} ({org}) — your account is attached for follow-up.
publicProjects.submittedAsNoOrg: Submitted as {name} — your account is attached for follow-up.
publicProjects.dedupeHeader: {count, plural, one {# similar request already exists — upvote it instead of creating a duplicate?} other {# similar requests already exist — upvote one instead of creating a duplicate?}}
publicProjects.continueAsNew: Not the same? Continue and submit as new →
publicProjects.confirmBody: Your request landed in the team’s triage queue. You’ll see it on the public roadmap once they review it. We’ll attribute it to your account.
publicProjects.upvotedBody: We added your upvote to the existing request. You’ll see it move on the public roadmap as the team works on it.
publicProjects.rateLimitedTitle: You’re submitting a little too fast
publicProjects.rateLimitedBody: Give it a moment and try again — your draft is saved.
publicProjects.submitErrorBody: Something went wrong. Your draft is saved — please try again.
publicProjects.boardEmptyBody: This project doesn’t have a public board yet. Check back soon.
publicProjects.boardProjectionNote: Assignees, estimates, and internal comments are hidden by the public projection — they aren’t fetched, not just hidden.
publicProjects.boardTruncatedNote: Showing the most recent {cap} cards. This is a public preview of a larger board.
publicProjects.workItemsSubtitle: A read-only view of this project’s public work items.
publicProjects.workItemsEmptyBody: There’s nothing on this project’s public board yet.
publicProjects.loadErrorBody: Something went wrong loading this view. Please try again.
publicProjects.roadmapEmptyBody: When requests are planned, they’ll show up here grouped by status.
publicProjects.commentsEmpty: No comments yet — be the first to weigh in.
publicProjects.commentError: Couldn’t post your comment. Please try again.
publicProjects.faqA1: Yes. This is a public project — anyone on the web can view it with no account, and it’s indexable by search engines. You only need to sign in to submit, upvote, or comment on requests.
publicProjects.faqQ2: How do I submit a request?
publicProjects.faqA2: Use the “Submit a request” button. You’ll be asked to sign in first (sign-in-to-act), then your bug or idea lands in the team’s triage queue and can appear on the public roadmap.
publicProjects.faqQ3: What’s hidden on the public view?
publicProjects.faqA3: Internal fields are stripped by the public projection: assignees, estimates, story points, and internal work-item comments are never sent to the public view — they’re absent from the data, not just hidden in the page.
publicProjects.treeSubtitle: A read-only, expandable view of this project’s public work items.
publicProjects.treeEmptyBody: There’s nothing on this project’s public board yet.
publicProjects.epicNotPublicBody: The project admin has kept this epic’s contents private.
publicProjects.detailNoChildrenBody: This epic has no stories or tasks yet.
publicProjects.editAdminHint: You manage this project. Hit “Edit page” to change the tagline, tags, and overview right here on the public page — what you edit is what visitors see (WYSIWYG). Only you and other admins see this.
publicProjects.editingBannerBody: Changes show live as you type — this is exactly what visitors will see. Save to publish, or Cancel to discard.
publicProjects.editErrTooLong: The overview is too long. Please shorten it.
publicProjects.editErrTaglineTooLong: That tagline is too long. Please shorten it.
publicProjects.editErrTagsInvalid: Those tags aren’t valid. Keep each one short, under the per-tag and total limits.
publicProjects.editErrNotAdmin: Only project admins can edit this page.
publicProjects.editErrGeneric: Something went wrong. Your edits are kept — please try again.
publicProjects.editGuardBody: You’ve edited the tagline, tags, or overview but haven’t saved. Leaving now discards those changes.

workItemActions.deleteCascadeBody: This permanently deletes {key} “{title}” and everything beneath it. This can’t be undone.
workItemActions.deleteLeafBody: This permanently deletes {key} “{title}”. It has no child items. This can’t be undone.
workItemActions.deleteCascadeCount: {count, plural, one {# descendant} other {# descendants}} will also be deleted — {breakdown}.
workItemActions.deleteHistoryRow: All comments, attachments, and activity history on these {count} items go with them.
workItemActions.deleteLeafHistoryRow: Its comments, attachments, and activity history are deleted too.
workItemActions.deleteLinksRow: Links from other items (blocks, relates to, duplicates) are removed.
workItemActions.deleteLiveWarningHead: Some of what’s beneath this isn’t archived.
workItemActions.deleteLiveWarningBody: Deleting also permanently removes {count, plural, one {# active work item that isn’t archived} other {# active work items that aren’t archived}} — {breakdown}. They’re still live on your boards and lists. (Archiving an item never archives its children.)
workItemActions.deleteAllArchivedRow: {count, plural, one {# descendant} other {# descendants}} will also be deleted — {breakdown}. All of them are already archived — nothing here is live on your boards.
workItemActions.archiveAltLead: Just want it out of the way?
workItemActions.archiveAltBody: Archive hides this item but keeps it and its history — and leaves its descendants live. You can restore it later.
workItemActions.deleteErrorBody: Couldn’t delete this item. Nothing was changed — the item and its descendants are intact. Check your connection and try again.
workItemActions.archiveErrorBody: Something went wrong. Check your connection and try again.

projectSquare.heroTitle: Explore public project plans built on Motir
projectSquare.heroLede: Browse real, public product roadmaps and project plans from teams building in the open — issues, boards, and sprints, free to read with no sign-up. Search by name, filter by topic, and sort by what's trending.
projectSquare.searchPlaceholder: Search public projects by name or topic…
projectSquare.paginationNote: keyset cursor · 24 per page · each page a real crawlable URL · no skip / no dupe
projectSquare.browseSubtitle: Topics sorted by their public-project count — each is its own crawlable landing page.
projectSquare.emptyBody: When a team makes a project public, it shows up here for everyone to discover. Check back soon.
projectSquare.noResultsBody: Try a different search or topic — or clear your filters to browse the full square.
projectSquare.errorBody: Something went wrong fetching public projects. Check your connection and try again.
projectSquare.faqLede: The project square is a public, crawlable directory of every project teams have chosen to build in the open on Motir — their real roadmaps, boards, and sprints, free to read with no account.
projectSquare.faqA1: It's a public directory of project plans built on Motir. Every listed project is public by choice — you can read its roadmap, board, and work items with no sign-up.
projectSquare.faqQ2: Are these projects free to view?
projectSquare.faqA2: Yes. Every project in the square is fully public and free to read — no account, no paywall. You only need an account to create your own project.
projectSquare.faqA3: Trending surfaces projects with recent upvotes and activity, Popular ranks by lifetime upvotes, and New lists the most recently published — each a shareable, crawlable URL.
projectSquare.footerTagline: The AI-native way to plan, track, and ship — explore what teams are building in the open.
projectSquare.metaDescription: Browse public product roadmaps and project plans from teams building in the open on Motir — issues, boards, and sprints, free to read with no sign-up.
projectSquare.metaDescriptionTopic: Public projects tagged {topic} — real roadmaps, boards, and sprints from teams building {topic} in the open on Motir.
projectSquare.topicLede: Public projects tagged {topic} — real roadmaps and plans from teams building in the open.
projectSquare.topicEmptyBody: No team has published a public project in this topic yet. Explore the full square instead.

aiUsage.title: Usage & cost
aiUsage.subtitle: Token cost for the {org} organization — credits spent planning across all its workspaces. Credits are a usage allotment shared across every model.

summary.monthlySpendSub: Credits debited per month, org-wide. Last 6 months.

aiUsage.affordance: Credits are an internal usage allotment — not a currency, and this is not a bill. One planning run debits credits by the tokens it consumed. Buying more credits and plan changes arrive with billing in a later release.

drill.note: Drilling re-scopes every panel — the balance share, the per-model breakdown and the run log — to the active level.

byModel.title: By model
byModel.subtitle: Where the org's credits went this month, per model. Tokens drive the credit cost; a costlier model debits more per token.

activity.title: Recent activity
activity.subtitle: Every planning run that debited credits, newest first. Filtered to the current scope ({scope}). Older runs load a page at a time.

member.yourShare: Your project's share. No org total, no other workspaces.
member.lockNote: Org-wide totals, the cross-workspace drill-up and the full run log are visible to organization owners and admins only. Ask an org admin for org-level usage.
member.noProjectDescription: You are a member of this organization but not yet part of a project. Project cost appears here once you join one.

lowBalance.title: Running low on credits.
lowBalance.body: {n} credits left — about {pct}% of this month's allotment. Planning still works; large generations may exhaust the balance. Buying more credits arrives with billing later.

outOfCredits.title: Planning is paused — you're out of credits
outOfCredits.body: The {org} organization has used all of this month's credits, so new planning runs are paused. Existing plans stay fully editable.
outOfCredits.passiveSlot: Buying more credits and changing your plan arrive with billing in a later release. This is where that option will appear.

states.emptyDescription: Once your team runs the AI planner, every run's credit cost shows up here — broken down by workspace, project and model.
states.errorDescription: Something went wrong fetching this organization's usage. The figures are temporarily unavailable — your credits are safe.

draftWithAi.gateTooltip: Connect Motir AI to draft with AI
draftWithAi.gateBody: Drafting runs on Motir Cloud; this self-hosted workspace has no connection yet.
draftWithAi.errorBody: Motir AI is unavailable right now. Any text generated so far is kept — try again.

landing.headline: What do you want to build?
landing.subhead: Describe your idea. Motir plans the whole project — brand, pricing, marketing and the code — tracks every kind of work, and ships the code for you.
landing.heroPlaceholder: A tool for freelancers to send invoices and track which clients have paid…
landing.heroHint: Free to start · no credit card
landing.footnote: Motir walks you through each step and writes up what it finds — you read it, react in the chat, and continue when it's right. Nothing's locked until your plan is generated.

steps.understandDesc: We chat to figure out what you're building and who it's for — and find the closest existing products.
steps.scopeDesc: We agree on what's in and out for the first version, and the key decisions behind it.
steps.planDesc: A complete to-do list Motir builds from — and tracks as a roadmap through every epic.

optional.summaryHint: — Motir adds these only when they fit your project
optional.worthDesc: A quick reality check — how big the market is and how hard it'll be to build.
optional.demandDesc: We check real demand and competitors — and can prove demand first with a marketing site before you build.
optional.designDesc: Pick how your product looks and feels — or skip it and use Motir's default.

connectGate.title: AI planning isn't configured
connectGate.body: Planning runs on Motir Cloud; this self-hosted workspace has no connection yet.

chat.gateNote: Continue = next step, not sign-off — nothing locks until your plan generates
chat.skipHint: A skip is a chat decision — made before a step is drafted. A write-up you're already reading has nothing to skip.
chat.validateLockNote: This sets the build order — Motir waits here until you choose.
chat.completeBody: Every step is reviewed. Turning it into a plan comes next.
chat.errorBody: Something went wrong reaching Motir. Try again.
chat.emptyTitle: Tell Motir what you want to build
chat.emptyBody: Describe your idea in the chat and Motir drafts each step for you to review.

replies.skip: Let's skip that step and move on.
replies.proveDemand: Let's prove demand first — build the marketing site and waitlist before the product.
replies.buildItAll: No — let's build the product straight away.

chat.noProjectBody: Pick or create a project first, then Motir can plan it with you.

canvas.title: Your project
canvas.caption: idea → checks → design → plan
canvas.validateFirstHint: Unproven for your exact take — validate first? (in the chat)

validate.title: One call before we plan
validate.body: The market check found real demand, but it's unproven for your exact take. Prove demand first — launch a small marketing site with a waitlist before building — or build straight away?
validate.blockedNote: Pick an option to continue — Motir waits here until you choose

design.title: Design your project's look
design.lead: Pick a theme, style, palette and type for your new project. This whole page previews your choice — and only this page. The rest of Motir stays exactly as it is.

theme.name: Theme
theme.help: Light, dark, or match the device.

note.system: Your project follows the device's light / dark setting.

style.name: Style
style.help: The shape and feel — silhouette, elevation, density.

palette.name: Palette
palette.help: The colour scheme the interface wears.

type.name: Typography
type.help: The typeface pairing for headings and body.

example.label: Live preview of your project in the chosen theme, style, palette and type

design.footerNote: This styles your project, not Motir — nothing locks, change it any time.
